problems.title: Polygon Perimeter
problems.prompt:

Add Euclidean distances between consecutive points and close the polygon from the final point back to the first. At least three points are supplied.

problems.title: Even Parity Bits
problems.prompt:
Append an even-parity bit to binary groups.

Background and required method:
For each eight-bit group append 0 when it already contains an even number of 1 bits, otherwise append 1 so the resulting nine bits have even parity.

problems.title: Successive Pi Approximations
problems.prompt:
Generate successive approximations of pi from an alternating series.

Background and required method:
The first approximation is 3. For n starting at 1, add or subtract 4 / ((2n)*(2n+1)*(2n+2)), alternating plus then minus. Return count cumulative approximations so each entry includes one more term than the previous entry.

Implement this interface:
def pi_approximations(count: int = 15) -> list[float]:

Parameters:
• count (int): Number of approximations to produce.

starterFiles.content:

    Args:

    Returns:
        list[float]: Generate successive approximations of pi from an alternating series.
    """
    pass

problems.title: Caesar Cipher
problems.prompt:
Apply a Caesar rotation to letters in text.

Rotate uppercase and lowercase letters independently with wraparound. Preserve spaces, digits, and punctuation unchanged. Negative shifts decode.

problems.title: Square Root with Newton's Method
problems.prompt:
Approximate a square root by repeatedly improving a guess.

Background and required method:
Follow Newton's method explicitly: for positive number, initialize guess to number/2. While abs(guess*guess-number) is greater than tolerance, replace guess with the average of guess and number/guess. Return the first guess that is good enough. Handle number=0 directly to avoid division by zero.

Implement this interface:
def newton_sqrt(number: float, tolerance: float = 1e-12) -> float:

Parameters:
• number (float): Non-negative value whose square root is required.
• tolerance (float): Maximum allowed value of abs(guess*guess-number).
problems.signature: def newton_sqrt(number: float, tolerance: float = 1e-12) -> float:

starterFiles.name: main.py
starterFiles.content:
def newton_sqrt(number: float, tolerance: float = 1e-12) -> float:
    """Approximate a square root by repeatedly improving a guess.

    Args:
        number (float): Non-negative value whose square root is required.
        tolerance (float): Maximum allowed value of abs(guess*guess-number).

    Returns:
        float: Approximate a square root by repeatedly improving a guess.
    """
    pass

problems.title: Greatest Common Divisor
problems.prompt:
Find the largest integer that divides two positive integers evenly.

Background and required method:
Use this required search algorithm: initialize candidate to min(a,b), then repeatedly decrease candidate by 1 while it fails to divide either input evenly. Return the first candidate that divides both.

• b (int): Second positive integer.

problems.title: Binary to Decimal
problems.prompt:
Convert a binary string to a decimal integer.

Process digits left to right, doubling the accumulated value before adding the next bit. Do not call int(binary, 2).

problems.title: Maximum Integer Simulation
problems.prompt:
Simulate the process of discovering new maximum values.

Background and required method:
Use a local random.Random(seed) to generate count integers from 1 through 100. Mark the first value as the initial maximum but do not count it as an update. For each later value, mark whether it establishes a strictly larger maximum. Return all (value,is_new_maximum) records, the final maximum, and the number of later updates.

Implement this interface:
def maximum_experiment(seed: int | None = None, count: int = 100) -> tuple[list[tuple[int, bool]], int, int]:

Parameters:
• seed (int | None): Optional seed for reproducible random values.
• count (int): Number of random integers to generate.
problems.signature: def maximum_experiment(seed: int | None = None, count: int = 100) -> tuple[list[tuple[int, bool]], int, int]:

examples.output: five marked values, final maximum, update count

starterFiles.name: main.py
starterFiles.content:
import random

def maximum_experiment(seed: int | None = None, count: int = 100) -> tuple[list[tuple[int, bool]], int, int]:
    """Simulate the process of discovering new maximum values.

    Args:
        seed (int | None): Optional seed for reproducible random values.
        count (int): Number of random integers to generate.

    Returns:
        tuple[list[tuple[int, bool]], int, int]: Simulate the process of discovering new maximum values.
    """
    pass

tests.name: count and range
tests.code: assert (lambda r: len(r[0])==100 and all(1<=v<=100 for v,_ in r[0]))(maximum_experiment(1)) == True

tests.name: summary matches records
tests.code: assert (lambda r: r[1]==max(v for v,_ in r[0]) and r[2]==sum(flag for _,flag in r[0][1:]))(maximum_experiment(3,20)) == True

problems.title: Coin-Flip Simulation
problems.prompt:
Simulate fair coin flips until three equal outcomes occur consecutively.

Background and required method:
For each trial, use a local random.Random(seed) stream to generate H or T with equal probability until HHH or TTT first appears. Record the complete outcome string and its flip count. Return all trial records and the average count. By default, perform 10 independent simulations.

Implement this interface:
def coin_flip_experiment(trials: int = 10, seed: int | None = None) -> tuple[list[tuple[str, int]], float]:

Parameters:
• trials (int): Number of independent streak simulations.
• seed (int | None): Optional seed for reproducible flips.
problems.signature: def coin_flip_experiment(trials: int = 10, seed: int | None = None) -> tuple[list[tuple[str, int]], float]:

examples.output: three outcome/count records and their average

starterFiles.name: main.py
starterFiles.content:
import random

def coin_flip_experiment(trials: int = 10, seed: int | None = None) -> tuple[list[tuple[str, int]], float]:
    """Simulate fair coin flips until three equal outcomes occur consecutively.

    Args:
        trials (int): Number of independent streak simulations.
        seed (int | None): Optional seed for reproducible flips.

    Returns:
        tuple[list[tuple[str, int]], float]: Simulate fair coin flips until three equal outcomes occur consecutively.
    """
    pass

tests.name: valid streak endings
tests.code: assert all(text.endswith('HHH') or text.endswith('TTT') for text,_ in coin_flip_experiment(5,2)[0]) == True
